task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'My page' personalization or configuration screen in Redmine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Redmine interface with the 'My page' or dashboard personalization view active.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'Latest news' block to be in the top position.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Latest news' block is visibly placed in the top-most section or zone of the page layout.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'Calendar' block to be in the left column.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Calendar' block is visibly placed in the left column or zone of the page layout.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'Spent time' block to be in the right column.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Spent time' block is visibly placed in the right column or zone of the page layout.

task_completion.description: Remove all other blocks so that only the three specified blocks are displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard displays exactly three blocks ('Latest news', 'Calendar', and 'Spent time'). No other blocks (such as 'Issues assigned to me', 'Reported issues', etc.) are visible on the page.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Redmine web GUI rather than using terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Redmine interface, clicking dropdowns, or dragging/dropping blocks.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Redmine application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a valid Redmine URL and the standard Redmine application interface is visible, not a local HTML file or text editor.

integrity.description: The layout changes were made through genuine application controls rather than browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots do not show the browser's 'Inspect Element' or Developer Tools open to manipulate the DOM directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: In Redmine 6.0, the 'Spent time' block is internally identified as 'timelog'. When verifying the right column, look for the block visually labeled 'Spent time'. Do not penalize the agent if system logs, URLs, or internal IDs refer to it as 'timelog' instead of 'spent_time'.
